title: aita for refusing to sleep in the same room as my fiancé?
text: we have been together for around 5 years. the past 3 years he has gained a little weight which is no big deal other than the fact he snores incredibly loud. his father has sleep apnea and i believe he does to. it sounds like a chainsaw cutting down trees that stops only for a few seconds and continues with vengeance. he refuses to see a doctor about it and he says i’m over exaggerating even when i show him a video of how loud it is. we tend to sleep separate because i have a habit of waking up in the middle of the night and it’s impossible to go back to sleep when he is snoring. i feel like the asshole because he’s upset i won’t sleep in the same room but i have told him countless of times to see a doctor as it sounds like he could die in his sleep, but he refuses. i believe it is putting a damper on our sex life because i can’t even cuddle and fall asleep with him without the thought of waking up and getting annoyed from his deafening snoring. i have tried earplugs, listening to music with headphones, etc but i don’t think that’s fair to me i should suffer. it is also extremely embarrassing to deal with considering we went on a cruise with my family and i had to share a room with my sister and her husband because you could hear him in the cabin hallway...am i the asshole? if i’m not how do i get him to see how frustrating it is for me :( i feel bad because i can’t imagine he feels rested by the way he sounds and tosses and turns.

storyboard.content: My fiancé and I have been together for around 5 years. Over the past 3 years he has gained some weight, and his father has sleep apnea. I believe my fiancé does too.
His snoring is incredibly loud — it sounds like a chainsaw cutting down trees that stops for only a few seconds and continues with a vengeance. You could hear him from the cabin hallway on a cruise.
He refuses to see a doctor about his snoring and says I’m overexaggerating, even when I show him a video of how loud it is.
I refuse to sleep in the same room as him. I have a habit of waking up in the middle of the night, and it’s impossible to go back to sleep when he is snoring.
I have told him countless times to see a doctor, as it sounds like he could die in his sleep, but he still refuses.
I have tried earplugs, listening to music with headphones, and other solutions, but I don’t think it’s fair that I should have to suffer.
It is putting a damper on our sex life because I can’t even cuddle and fall asleep with him without dreading being woken up by his snoring.
On a cruise with my family, I had to share a room with my sister and her husband because you could hear his snoring in the hallway. It was extremely embarrassing.
